response.justification: The candidate is a clearly senior, versatile engineer with genuine tooling interests — including direct IDE development experience on NI LabWindows/CVI and multiple self-built developer tools (test runners, assertion libraries). However, the role's core requirements are deep Java/Kotlin and JVM expertise plus solid Spring ecosystem experience, and the resume shows a career centered on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, with only incidental Java exposure and no Kotlin or Spring at all. The stack mismatch on the hard requirements outweighs the strong general engineering background.
strengths: Prior IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and authored developer tooling (test runner, assertion library, web server library)
15+ years of broad senior experience across web, backend, embedded, and high-throughput low-latency systems
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) and community teaching/workshop track record
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring experience — both are core required qualifications
Java appears only peripherally (side projects at one employer), far from the 'strong expertise with deep JVM understanding' required
Recent work is TypeScript/Node and AI-agent tooling, not enterprise JVM frameworks, build tools, or application servers
